content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 14, 2017

Rebecca L. Moon
Sr. Assistant City Attorney
456 W. Olive Avenue
P O Box 3707
Sunnyvale, CA 94088

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-17-082

Dear Ms. Moon:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Sunnyvale Planning
Commissioner Sue Harrison regarding her duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of
interest or Section 1090.

QUESTION

May Commissioner Harrison participate in decisions related to the approval of a new 108-
unit apartment complex proposed for property approximately 684 feet from her home?

CONCLUSION

Based on the facts presented, it does not appear the project will have a material financial
effect on the value of the commissioner's home, therefore the commissioner may participate in the
decision.

FACTS

The Planning Commission will be considering an application to build a 108-unit rental
apartment complex on a 2.05-acre site on El Camino Real and Poplar Avenue. The proposed
apartment complex will consist of one five-story building facing El Camino Real (four stories
above ground plus one underground level of parking) and one three-story building facing Poplar

_______________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise
indicated.

File No. A-17-082
Page No. 2

Avenue. Twenty percent of the units will be designated for very low income tenants. The site was
formerly a 44-unit mobile home park (closed late 2016) known as Nick's Trailer Court at 1008 E.
El Camino Real. The Project site also includes demolition of a duplex residence at 1314-1320
Poplar Avenue.

The Project's Initial Study found no significant impacts that cannot be reduced to a less than
significant level with appropriate mitigation. A Mitigated Negative Declaration was prepared and
filed on March 24, 2017.

• With respect to transportation and traffic, the Project was found to generate 32 net new
vehicle trips in morning (AM) peak hours and 56 net new trips in afternoon (PM) peak hours
compared to the prior uses on the site.

• A Transportation Operations Analysis of 11 nearby intersections determined that all
intersections will continue to operate at acceptable levels and no queuing deficiencies will
occur.

• You noted it did not appear that the Project will have any impact on Chair Harrison's
parking, views, privacy, noise levels or air quality.

• The Project will not substantially change the intensity of use in the surrounding area, which
is already highly developed and includes single family and multifamily residential
neighborhoods as well as the busy commercial and retail corridor on El Camino Real.

• The uses immediately adjacent to the project site include a dentist office, shopping center,
apartments, townhomes and single family homes. There is an auto dealership and drive-
through Starbucks on the other side of El Camino Real.

• The project will require re-zoning the site of the duplex residence from R-1/ECR (single-
family homes) to C-2/ECR (commercial). The closed mobile home park is currently zoned
C-2/ECR. Residential development is allowed in C-2/ECR zones with a Special
Development Permit (use permit).

Commissioner Harrison is the owner of a single-family residence approximately 684 feet
southwest of the Project. Her residence is separated from the Project site by approximately two
residential blocks.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise
using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. Under Section 87103(b), an official has a financial interest in a decision within
the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect on any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth
$2,000 or more. You ask specifically about potential effects on Planning Commissioner Harrison's
real property.

File No. A-17-082
Page No. 3

Foreseeability and Materiality

Under the Act, an effect on an interest is presumed foreseeable if the interest is explicitly
involved in the decision, and an interest is explicitly involved in the decision if the interest is a
named party, or subject of, the decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) Additionally, an interest in real
property is explicitly involved in a decision if it is a decision described in Regulation 18702.2(a)(1)-
(6), none of which apply to your facts. Since the Commissioner's residence is not "explicitly
involved" in the decisions relating to the Project, the financial effect is foreseeable if the effect of
the decision on the commissioner's residence can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more
than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).)

Regulation 18702.2(a) sets forth the materiality standards for determining whether the
reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a governmental decision on a public official's financial
interest in real property is material. Relevant to your inquiry and the facts presented, the financial
effect on an official's real property interest in his residence is material if the decision:

• Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially altering traffic
levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property surrounding the official's real
property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or air quality, including odors, or any other
factors that would affect the market value of the real property parcel in which the official
has a financial interest (Regulation 18702.2(a)(10)).

• Would cause a reasonably prudent person using due care and consideration under the
circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a nature that its
reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of the official's property.
(Regulation 18702.2(a)(12).)

Your facts suggest that the Project will not materially affect the value of the
Commissioner's home. You noted that the project will generate only 32 net new vehicle trips in AM
peak hours and 56 net new trips in afternoon PM peak hours and that the Initial Study and Traffic
Operations Analysis found no significant impact on the levels of service or queuing deficiencies at
nearby intersections.

Moreover, while the transition of the trailer park to a 108-unit rental apartment complex will
increase the intensity of use of the property and could impact the visual character of neighboring
properties² based on its height, it does not appear there will be a significant change in the intensity
of use or character of the neighborhood. The surrounding area is highly developed and includes
single family and multifamily residential neighborhoods as well as the busy commercial and retail
corridor on El Camino Real. For example, an existing middle school operates just to the southeast
of the Commissioner's home, and numerous businesses and restaurants line El Camino Real (on

___________________________
² The Notice of Intent to Adopt a Mitigated Negative Declaration states that "Construction of the project will
alter the visual character of the site and neighboring properties, which includes one and two-story residential structures.
The existing buildings consist of one and two-story homes, including mobile homes, a single-family home and a
duplex."

File No. A-17-082
Page No. 4

both sides of the street) between the commission's home and the project site.³ In addition, the
Commissioner's home is buffered from the site by two residential neighborhoods and a small
shopping center. Therefore, we agree that it does not appear that the Project will have a material
financial effect on the value of the Commissioner Harrison's property.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

_______________

¹ The Notice of Intent to Adopt a Mitigated Negative Declaration describes the surrounding area as follows: "A
dentist office and five-unit apartment building are located west of the main portion of the project site (north of the
duplex) and a shopping center is located to the east-all zoned C-2/ECR. Across El Camino Real to the north of the
project site are commercial uses, including an auto dealership and a drive-through cafe. The adjacent properties to the
south include single-family homes and townhomes. The single-family home adjacent to 1314-1320 Poplar Ave. to the
south is zoned R- 1/ECR and the other single-family homes are zoned R-1, and not subject to the Precise Plan for El
Camino Real. The adjacent townhomes are zoned R-3/PD (Medium Density Residential/Planned Development)."

unit apartment complex proposed for property approximately 684 feet from her home?

CONCLUSION: Based on the facts presented, it does not appear the project will have a material financial
